channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: Russia Offers India Two Squadrons of Su-57 by 2030-31 Amid Production Ramp-Up and Engine Upgrade Plans
item.description: India is evaluating a fresh fifth-generation fighter proposal from Russia, with sources indicating that Moscow has offered to supply two squadrons of Su-57 stealth fighters—approximately 36 to 40 aircraft—by 2030, provided a deal is concluded by the end of this year. The timeline shared with Indian stakeholders suggests that initial deliveries could begin as early as late 2027 or 2028, with full induction completed by the end of the decade.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Advances Passive IRST Sensor Fusion to Counter Stealth Threats, Powers Tejas Mk2 and Super Sukhoi Upgrades
item.description: India is accelerating the development of passive Infrared Search and Track (IRST) fusion as a cornerstone of its next-generation airborne sensor architecture, marking a doctrinal shift toward low-observable detection without electromagnetic emissions. This capability is central to countering stealth aircraft, particularly in contested environments where traditional radar performance is degraded or actively targeted by electronic warfare systems.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India to ‘Hardwire’ Indigenous Weapon Integration into 114 Rafale Deal via ICD Clause
item.description: India is taking a firm and technically strategic approach in its proposed ?3.25 lakh crore Rafale fighter deal, ensuring that indigenously developed weapons and missile systems can be integrated into the 114 aircraft planned for procurement. According to sources, the Ministry of Defence (MoD) will mandate the inclusion of an Interface Control Document (ICD) as a binding element within the government-to-government contract with France.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Set to Issue RFP for 114 Rafale F4/F5 Fighters with ‘Made in India’ Clause Next Month
item.description: India is preparing to take a decisive step in its long-pending multi-role fighter acquisition program, with the Ministry of Defence (MoD) expected to issue the Request for Proposal (RFP) to Dassault Aviation next month for the procurement of 114 Rafale fighter jets. This follows the Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) approval granted on February 12, which cleared the way for one of the largest fighter deals in recent years.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Pakistani Expert Panic after India’s PFBR Milestones Warns of Vertical Proliferation Risks
item.description: Ambassador Qazi M. Khalilullah (R), Executive Director of the Center for International Strategic Studies (CISSS), has expressed deep concern over the Director General of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) praising India’s recent achievement in attaining criticality of its Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) at Kalpakkam. The milestone, reached on April 6, 2026, marks a significant step in India’s three-stage nuclear program aimed at enhancing fuel sustainability and long-term energy security. However, Khalilullah described the IAEA chief’s endorsement as surprising, if not shocking, given that the PFBR operates entirely outside IAEA safeguards.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: MIDHANI Secures Airworthiness Certification for Aero-Engine Alloys, Boosting India’s Indigenous Materials Capability
item.description: India’s strategic materials ecosystem has achieved a significant breakthrough as Mishra Dhatu Nigam Limited (MIDHANI) secured airworthiness certification for multiple advanced alloys and mill forms intended for aero-engine applications. This milestone marks a major step forward in strengthening India’s self-reliance in high-performance materials critical to aerospace and defence sectors.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Kalam Labs Plans Stratospheric Long-Endurance Airship for Persistent Surveillance at 50,000 ft
item.description: India’s emerging aerospace startup ecosystem is increasingly exploring high-altitude platforms designed to provide long-duration surveillance and communication capabilities. Kalam Labs, through its Stratospheric Aerial Research Lab, is developing a concept for a Stratospheric Long-Endurance Airship (SLEA) capable of hovering at altitudes above 50,000 feet to support persistent intelligence, surveillance, and reconnaissance missions.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: ISRO has successfully conducted the second integrated air drop test (IADT-02) for the upcoming Gaganyaan mission
item.description: Space agency Isro has successfully conducted the second integrated air drop test (IADT-02) for the upcoming Gaganyaan mission at the space station in Andhra Pradesh’s Sriharikota.The system is essential to ensure safe recovery of the crew module — the capsule in which astronauts sit during a human flight — during re-entry and landing.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Operation Sindoor: Army Delayed Strike At Terror Camps To Respect Namaz, Reveals Gen Dwivedi
item.description: Chief of Army Staff General Upendra Dwivedi has revealed that the Indian Army deliberately delayed a planned strike during Operation Sindoor to avoid disrupting ‘namaz’ at a terrorist camp, underlining what he described as a conscious effort to balance military objectives with humanitarian considerations.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian Army Clears Col Shrikant Purohit For Promotion To Brigadier Rank
item.description: The Indian Army has cleared Colonel Shrikant Purohit for promotion to the rank of Brigadier, marking a significant development in a long-running service dispute linked to his career progression and earlier legal proceedings in the 2008 Malegaon blast case.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Indian rupee soars to best day in over a decade as RBI clamps down on speculation
item.description: The Indian rupee surged on Thursday after the central bank tightened the screws on speculative bets against the currency, with the Asian unit having its best day since 2013, when policymakers had also resorted to extraordinary measures to stabilise markets.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India must rethink reliance on West Asia after current energy shock: ONGC
item.description: As the world scrambles to recover from the biggest energy shock in decades triggered by the West Asia conflict, India should be cautious about relying on traditional supply assumptions, particularly its proximity to the Middle East for sourcing oil and gas, ONGC Chairman and CEO Arun Kumar Singh said on Friday.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India-US Ties Strengthen As Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri Meets FBI Chief Kash Patel
item.description: India and the United States expanded their strategic engagement with Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri holding a key meeting with Secretary of State Marco Rubio and separate talks with senior US officials, including the FBI Director Kash Patel, covering defence, counter-terrorism and regional security.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India Launches First Quantum Computer Testing Facility In Amaravati
item.description: Marking World Quantum Day on April 14, Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N. Chandrababu Naidu will formally launch India’s first indigenous Quantum computer testing facility at SRM University, Amaravati. He will also virtually inaugurate the Quantum facility centre at Medha Towers in Gannavaram near Vijayawada.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: India, Bhutan Discuss Strengthening Energy Security And Hydropower Cooperation
item.description: Union Minister for Power Manohar Lal Khattar held a meeting with Bhutan’s Prime Minister Tshering Tobgay on Thursday, discussing strengthening energy security, hydropower cooperation, cultural and people-to-people ties between the two nations. “Pleased to welcome Manohar Lal Khattar today. We discussed strengthening energy security and hydropower cooperation to further the enduring friendship between Bhutan and India,” Tobgay said on X. Union Minister Khattar expressed India’s commitment to partnering with Bhutan at every level.
item.dc:creator: admin
